moduleBenefitmonitoring:
Live Monitoring tracks every keyword position daily and instantly shows you what moved. You get a full ranking table with current position, change since last check, and a sparkline showing 7-day trend — all without opening separate reports.

Why it matters: Google updates its algorithm thousands of times a year. Without daily tracking, you could lose a top-3 position and not notice for weeks, costing you significant traffic. This module catches drops the same day they happen so you can investigate immediately — whether it's an algorithm update, a competitor pushing new content, or a technical issue on your site.

Practical use cases: Set up keyword groups by landing page to see which pages perform best. Track branded vs. non-branded keywords separately. Use position distribution charts to see how many keywords sit in top 3, top 10, and top 100 — then set monthly goals to move keywords up from each bucket. The movement trend chart reveals if your overall SEO trajectory is improving or declining week over week.

Key metrics you get: exact position per keyword, position change (daily/weekly/monthly), sparkline mini-charts, position distribution breakdown, net movement trend over time, per-keyword SERP URL tracking.
moduleBenefitkeyword-map:
Keyword Map transforms your flat keyword list into a visual cluster map organized by topic and search intent. Instead of seeing 200 disconnected phrases, you see which topics you dominate, which ones need more content, and where keywords might be cannibalizing each other.

Why it matters: Google's algorithm increasingly rewards topical authority — sites that cover a subject comprehensively. A flat keyword list tells you nothing about topical coverage. The cluster map reveals if you have 40 keywords about 'SEO audit' but only 2 about 'technical SEO' — exposing a massive content gap that competitors may already be filling.

Practical use cases: Identify cannibalization where two pages compete for the same keyword cluster. Plan content calendars by seeing which clusters need pillar articles vs. supporting posts. Assign intent labels (informational, commercial, transactional, navigational) to understand where users are in the buying funnel. Use the map to present content strategy to clients or stakeholders in a visual format that non-SEO people understand instantly.

Key metrics you get: topic clusters with keyword count, search intent distribution, cluster volume and difficulty averages, cannibalization warnings, content gap indicators per cluster, visual bubble map sized by traffic potential.
moduleBenefitvisibility:
Visibility Score condenses all your keyword positions into a single number from 0 to 100 that represents how much organic search real estate you own. A score of 70 means you capture about 70% of available clicks for your tracked keywords.

Why it matters: Individual keyword positions fluctuate daily, making it hard to tell if your overall SEO is improving. The visibility score smooths this noise into one metric that stakeholders understand instantly. Instead of explaining 'we went from position 7 to 5 on 12 keywords but dropped from 3 to 6 on 3 others,' you say 'visibility improved 4.2 points this month.'

Practical use cases: Track visibility weekly to measure ROI of SEO campaigns. Compare your score against competitors to benchmark your market share. Use the discovered keywords feature to find phrases Google already ranks you for that you aren't actively targeting — these are low-hanging fruit where minimal optimization could push you from page 2 to page 1.

Key metrics you get: overall visibility score with trend, score change over 7/30/90 days, discovered keywords with position and volume, top 3 / top 10 / top 100 keyword counts, competitor visibility comparison, estimated organic traffic value based on positions and CPC data.
moduleBenefitcompetitors:
Competitor Overview puts your domain side-by-side with up to 10 rivals, comparing rankings, keyword overlap, backlink profiles, and content strategies in one dashboard.

Why it matters: SEO doesn't happen in a vacuum. Your rankings depend on what competitors do. If a rival publishes 20 articles targeting your keywords, your positions will drop even if you did nothing wrong. This module lets you see competitor moves in real-time and respond proactively rather than reactively.

Practical use cases: Identify which competitors consistently outrank you and on which keywords — then analyze their content to understand why. Track competitor position changes to detect when they're investing in a new content area. Use the keyword overlap analysis to find phrases where you and a competitor both rank poorly — these are opportunities where neither site has established dominance. Monitor competitor backlink acquisition to know when they're running outreach campaigns.

Key metrics you get: head-to-head position comparison per keyword, keyword overlap matrix (shared, unique to you, unique to competitor), competitor position change trends, backlink radar comparing link profiles, content analysis showing competitor top-performing pages, keyword gap table highlighting uncontested opportunities.
moduleBenefitcontent-gaps:
Content Gap Analysis cross-references your keyword footprint with competitors to find phrases they rank for that you don't cover at all. Each gap represents an article or page you could create to capture traffic competitors already enjoy.

Why it matters: The fastest way to grow organic traffic is to target keywords where demand exists but you have zero presence. Creating content for these gaps means you're entering proven markets — competitors have already validated that these keywords drive traffic. Your risk is lower than targeting unproven keywords.

Practical use cases: Filter gaps by search volume to prioritize high-traffic opportunities first. Sort by keyword difficulty to find easy wins where you can rank quickly. Cross-reference with intent data to focus on transactional keywords that drive revenue rather than informational keywords that just attract visitors. Build monthly content calendars directly from the gap analysis — each gap becomes a content brief with target keyword, volume, difficulty, and competitor URLs to study.

Key metrics you get: gap keywords sorted by volume and difficulty, which competitors rank for each gap keyword and in what position, gap coverage matrix (you vs. competitor 1, 2, 3), intent classification per gap keyword, estimated traffic potential if you rank in top 10, suggested content type based on current SERP composition.
moduleBenefitlink-building:
Link Building surfaces domains that link to your competitors but not to you, then helps you organize and track outreach to those prospects. Each prospect shows domain authority, link type, and current outreach status.

Why it matters: Backlinks remain one of Google's top 3 ranking factors. But finding quality link prospects manually is tedious — you'd need to analyze every competitor's backlink profile, filter out low-quality domains, and cross-reference to remove sites that already link to you. This module automates that entire workflow and presents a curated list of high-value targets.

Practical use cases: Sort prospects by domain authority to focus on the highest-impact links first. Filter by link type (guest post, resource page, mention, directory) to match your team's outreach capabilities. Track which prospects you've contacted, which responded, and which converted into actual links. Set monthly link acquisition targets and measure progress against them. Export prospect lists for outreach tools like Pitchbox or BuzzStream.

Key metrics you get: prospect domain authority, link type classification, competitor that earned the link, estimated link value, outreach status tracking (not contacted / contacted / in conversation / link earned / rejected), link velocity (new links earned per week), backlink gap analysis showing total available vs. earned prospects.
moduleBenefitbacklinks:
Backlink Profile gives you a complete inventory of every site linking to yours — total backlinks, unique referring domains, dofollow vs. nofollow ratio, anchor text distribution, and link acquisition velocity over time.

Why it matters: Your backlink profile directly influences how Google perceives your site's authority. A sudden spike in low-quality links could trigger a penalty. A gradual loss of links could erode your rankings. This module acts as your backlink health dashboard — you see problems before they impact rankings.

Practical use cases: Monitor dofollow ratio to ensure most links pass SEO value. Track velocity charts to see if link acquisition is accelerating or slowing. Identify toxic links from spam domains that could hurt your rankings — then submit them to Google's disavow tool. Analyze anchor text distribution to check if it looks natural (Google penalizes over-optimized anchor text). Compare your link profile against competitors to understand how many more links you need to compete.

Key metrics you get: total backlinks and referring domains, dofollow/nofollow/ugc/sponsored breakdown, link acquisition and loss velocity over 7/30/90 days, TLD and country distribution, platform type distribution (blogs, forums, news, directories), anchor text analysis, domain rating of linking sites, link quality score, historical backlink growth chart.
moduleBenefiton-site:
Site Health Audit crawls your website and flags every technical SEO issue that could be hurting your rankings — from broken links and missing meta tags to slow page speed, duplicate content, and crawl errors.

Why it matters: Even the best content won't rank if Google can't properly crawl and index your pages. A single noindex tag on an important page can remove it from search results entirely. Slow load times directly impact Core Web Vitals scores, which Google uses as a ranking signal. This module catches these issues before they cost you traffic.

Practical use cases: Run monthly audits and track the health score trend to ensure your technical foundation is improving. Prioritize fixes by severity — critical issues (like blocked pages or server errors) first, then warnings (missing alt text, long titles), then optimizations. Use page speed analysis to find which pages need performance optimization. Check for duplicate content and canonical tag issues that might cause keyword cannibalization. Monitor robots.txt and sitemap.xml health to ensure Google can discover all your pages.

Key metrics you get: overall site health score (0-100), critical / warning / passed issue counts, broken link inventory, missing meta tags list, page speed scores per URL, Core Web Vitals assessment, duplicate content detection, canonical tag audit, robots.txt validation, sitemap coverage analysis, mobile usability issues, HTTPS and security checks.
moduleBenefitinsights:
AI Insights automatically analyzes all your SEO data — keyword positions, backlink profile, competitor landscape, site audit results — and generates prioritized action recommendations. Instead of you digging through 5 different dashboards, the AI tells you what matters most right now.

Why it matters: Most SEO tools show you data but leave interpretation to you. With hundreds of keywords, dozens of competitors, and thousands of backlinks to analyze, it's easy to miss the most impactful action. The AI acts as an expert analyst that processes everything simultaneously and surfaces the highest-ROI recommendations.

Practical use cases: Start each week by reading the latest insights to know exactly where to focus. Share AI-generated recommendations with your content team to align SEO and editorial priorities. Use priority labels (high/medium/low) to triage your SEO backlog. Track which recommendations you've implemented and measure their impact over time. The AI considers cross-module data — for example, it might recommend creating content for a gap keyword where you also have strong backlink potential from your link building prospects.

Key metrics you get: prioritized action list (high/medium/low impact), recommendation categories (keywords, links, technical, content), estimated effort per action, expected impact description, cross-module correlations, trend-based alerts (e.g., 'competitor X gained 50 links this month targeting your top keyword'), weekly digest of most important changes across all modules.
moduleBenefitai-research:
AI Keyword Research goes beyond traditional keyword tools by using artificial intelligence to discover opportunities you'd never find with seed keyword expansion alone. It analyzes your niche, identifies emerging topics, and surfaces long-tail variations with real traffic potential.

Why it matters: Traditional keyword research starts with a seed keyword and expands it — but this approach only finds what you already know about. AI research explores your market from multiple angles: analyzing competitor content, identifying question patterns from search queries, detecting seasonal trends, and finding topically related keywords that don't share the same root phrase.

Practical use cases: Enter your domain or a broad topic area, and the AI returns keyword clusters you haven't considered. Discover question-based keywords ('how to...', 'why does...', 'what is...') that work perfectly for FAQ sections and featured snippets. Find low-competition long-tail keywords where a single well-optimized article could rank in the top 3 within weeks. Identify emerging trends in your niche before competitors notice them. Add any discovered keyword to your monitoring with one click — no manual data entry.

Key metrics you get: AI-discovered keyword suggestions with volume and difficulty, search intent classification, trend direction (rising/stable/declining), competitive density, related topic clusters, question keywords for FAQ targeting, estimated ranking difficulty for your specific domain, one-click add to monitoring.
moduleBenefitstrategy:
Strategy Builder creates a comprehensive, step-by-step SEO action plan customized to your domain's current data. The AI analyzes your rankings, backlinks, site health, competitor landscape, and content gaps — then generates a prioritized roadmap with specific tasks and estimated timelines.

Why it matters: Knowing your data is only half the battle — the other half is deciding what to do about it and in what order. Most SEO practitioners waste time on low-impact tasks while ignoring quick wins. The Strategy module eliminates guesswork by producing a data-driven action plan ranked by expected ROI.

Practical use cases: Generate a strategy at the start of each quarter to align your team. Share the action plan with clients to demonstrate a clear path forward. Track task completion to measure execution velocity. Use the strategy as a framework for monthly SEO reports — each completed task is proof of progress. The AI adapts recommendations based on your domain's specific strengths and weaknesses — a site with strong backlinks but weak content gets a content-focused strategy, while a content-rich site with few links gets a link-building-focused plan.

Key metrics you get: prioritized task list with effort estimates, task categories (content, technical, links, optimization), completion tracking with checkboxes, estimated timeline per task, strategy progress percentage, before/after impact measurement for completed tasks, automatic re-prioritization as your data changes.
moduleBenefitkeyword-analysis:
Keyword Analysis provides an in-depth report for any individual keyword — search volume, keyword difficulty, CPC value, SERP feature analysis, and a breakdown of the top 10 ranking pages with their metrics.

Why it matters: Before investing time and money into targeting a keyword, you need to understand what you're up against. A keyword with 10K monthly volume sounds great — but if the top 10 results are all DA 90+ sites with thousands of backlinks, your chances of ranking are slim. This module gives you the intelligence to make informed targeting decisions.

Practical use cases: Evaluate potential keywords before adding them to your monitoring list. Analyze what type of content ranks for a keyword (blog posts, product pages, videos, tools) to inform your content format. Study the top 10 competitors for a keyword to understand what it takes to rank — word count, backlink count, domain authority. Compare keyword difficulty across similar phrases to find the easiest path to page 1. Use CPC data to estimate the commercial value of organic traffic from each keyword.

Key metrics you get: monthly search volume with trend, keyword difficulty score (0-100), average CPC and competition level, SERP feature presence (featured snippets, PAA, image pack, video, local pack), top 10 ranking pages with DA/backlinks/word count, content type distribution in SERPs, estimated organic CTR per position, related keywords with metrics, SERP volatility indicator.
moduleBenefitgenerators:
Schema & Content Generators produce ready-to-use technical SEO assets — JSON-LD structured data markup, LLMs.txt files for AI crawler instructions, and optimized meta descriptions for your pages.

Why it matters: Structured data is no longer optional for competitive SEO. Google uses it to generate rich snippets (star ratings, FAQs, how-to steps, product prices) that dramatically increase click-through rates — some studies show CTR improvements of 20-30% for pages with rich snippets vs. plain blue links. The LLMs.txt generator ensures your site communicates properly with AI crawlers from ChatGPT, Perplexity, and others — an increasingly important traffic source.

Practical use cases: Generate Organization, Product, FAQ, Article, or HowTo schema for any page and paste it directly into your HTML. Create a LLMs.txt file that tells AI crawlers which pages are most important and how to cite your content. Generate optimized meta descriptions that include target keywords and compelling CTAs. Test generated schema against Google's Rich Results Test before deployment. Keep your structured data updated as your business information changes.

Key metrics you get: generated JSON-LD schema with validation, LLMs.txt file content, meta description suggestions with character count, schema type recommendations per page type, rich snippet eligibility indicator, copy-to-clipboard functionality for instant deployment.
moduleBenefitsettings:
Domain Settings control how your monitoring engine works — which search engine to query, what geographic location and language to use for position checks, and how often to refresh data.

Why it matters: Position data is only useful if it reflects the market you actually serve. A site targeting Polish customers needs position checks from google.pl in Polish, not google.com in English. Setting the wrong location could show you ranking #3 for a keyword when your actual target audience sees you at #15. Getting this right is fundamental to every other module's accuracy.

Practical use cases: Set your primary market's search engine (google.pl, google.de, google.com, etc.) and location for accurate local results. Choose the language that matches your content for proper SERP alignment. Adjust refresh frequency based on your needs — daily for active campaigns, weekly for maintenance monitoring, on-demand for cost optimization. If you serve multiple markets, configure the primary one here and use additional domains for secondary markets.

Key metrics you get: configured search engine, target location and language, refresh frequency schedule, estimated monthly API usage based on keyword count and frequency, last refresh timestamp, data freshness indicator.

moduleBenefitalerts: [FR] Custom Alert Rules let you define automated monitoring conditions for your domains. Set thresholds for position drops, top-N exits, backlink losses, and visibility changes. When conditions are met, the system creates alerts and sends in-app notifications so you never miss critical SEO changes.
